content:
11 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Generated by ChatGPTWhat if you’re rejecting AI to use as your search engine, and after you accept it you now become an AI co-founder? Had a bad experience and rethought my approach to constructing things.
I thought that I knew what AI was.
This product had been available in the market for some months. Programming with email, summarization and code snippets. I felt comfortable. Confident, even. He could cook in a restaurant, he thought he had enough TV shows on cooking.
So I had the idea I had been putting off for the past 6 months, in front of a half brewed cup of coffee and I gave it a go!
Not play with it. Test it. Brutally.
I built a startup from ground up, with a week of help from an AI agent, is now ready for contest. I literally built a startup from scratch with an AI agent and already it’s ready for contest in just a week! No previous researches have been saved here. It is not possible to upload a pitch deck at this time. No manufacturer contacts. Nothing.
Time limit: One idea for each sentence.
In the next seven days, a relatively inconsequential, but consequential event took place that changed my thinking about starting a business.

My notes app was the place I recorded the concept which is The Idea That Kept Sitting In My Notes App.
I’d been considering it for a while, it was a video studio for 1 man all-in-one solution.
It’s not a large ring light. Not an accessory of the mirrorless camera. Something small. Jacket-pocket small. Compact foldable teleprompter, mic mounting clip and fill light all in one case for all your needs.
I’m going to call it Creator Dock.
The price they desired was $79. The audience: anyone who films content alone in their bedroom or hotel room, and never looks professionally put together due to the lack of suitable gear, and not the lack of talent.
Great idea? Maybe. But ideas without action, are nothing. And, “execution” in hardware relates to supply chains and supplier negotiations, the minimum order quantities, and months of unopened emails.
This was the idea that was caught in my notes app, however, due to the friction.
I just daren’t stop talking about the new Axio Work AI tool so I did what anyone else would do and opened it up! I did not open the Axio Work AI tool until I had had some conversation about it!

AI has made a map before I’ve even had a cup of coffee.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Generated by ChatGPTThe AI had done one map even before I’d made it to my cup of coffee.
The Axio Work interface shows a 7 day roadmap showing day to day task blocks and milestone labels such as Idea Lock, Proposal Draft, Application Submit and more.
The first easy thing that I did was to… I wanted to hear from the agent some sort of realistic 7-day plan to prepare for the competition @ Alibaba.com’s Co-Create Pitch.
It is an international start-up competition that is conducted by an international team. Total total prize of more than a million dollars. A $200,000 grand prize. Regional Finals at Los Angeles & London. I had a raw idea which I wanted to get on a certain track and I named it “0 to 1”.
I was out of here after only a couple of minutes!
Day 1 : Idea validation and roadmap.
Day 2 : Formatting the proposal.
Day 3 : Finalising the proposal.
Day 4 Supply chain sourcing
Day 5 Supply chain sourcing
Day 6 Application finalisation & submission.
Day 7: Edge Cases and Review.
It looked simple. Clean. Organized.
I was surprised by just one thing: It didn’t just give me a checklist! Organized his day around a ‘product per day’ agenda and explained to me what was important to them time, cost, production difficulty.
At the time I knew that I was going to be using a passive device.

Days 2 & 3: It’s not just my idea. It Pushed Back.
This is the one thing that I didn’t expect.
I had hoped that he would take my idea, nod his head, create a set of numbers, say the ballpark size of the market and then feel good for awhile, and then go on.
But it wasn’t at all like that.
The agent was engaging in questioning a client.
What would you expect to be the margin of error on the US$79 face value?
Who is going to be the first to get this customer: creators, who are purchasing gear or beginners who do not know which gear to buy?
What is something that you will have to overcome with Creator Dock that will be more difficult in 6 months?
These questions were NOT “padding” questions. These are the types of questions that investors ask at the first meeting. The questions you are able to ask that show that you don’t know your business and that you are interested in your idea.
I had to think. Genuinely think.
They responded to me and the agent made a proposal of the following:

• Market Sizing is a creator economy-focused area of Solos. Market Sizing is a creator economy area of Solos.

• A comparison to the mobile solutions that are available. This is in comparison with the existing mobile equipment solutions.

• Innovative approaches to getting products to the market around creator communities and pre-orders.

• A sensible estimate of the mark up of the parts at the retail level.
This should only take 30 minutes.
I would have spent 2 weeks on creating a document and this guy would take 30 minutes. Not for the lack of desire to write! That’s because it may be paralyzing to attempt to remember all the variables at once on one blank page.
Why this is important: It’s actually the blank page problem. Most ideas are not bad ideas, they are just ideas which are not yet activated and most of the time, they simply don’t have the activation energy. This agent brought it down to an almost negligible value.

The Old Way vs. The New Way
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Generated by ChatGPTLet’s take a few moments to imagine what it must have been like to create artwork in a different manner.
The traditional way: Go to a template and read it, type some bullet items, and then shut the laptop and declare, I will write it tomorrow. Two weeks pass. The notion goes to sleep.
The new way is to make conversation. You answer questions. All of a sudden, something arises in the present moment, in front of you, that is created for your real answers. If it implies something that is not right, you don’t believe it. You refine it. You own it.
It’s more akin to dealing with a person who’s experienced it and has no ego about it as opposed to software.

After all these days, on Day #4, my friend said to me: “The whole thing is really quite funny.”
Actually, a friend of my’s told me that on Day 4 (which is now months after I applied the exercise), everything is very funny.
To see the supplier results in Axio Work for the search term “teleprompter beam splitter glass”, use the image above as a visual aid. The above diagram is meant to be used as a visual aid when presenting the supplier results in Axio Work for the search term “teleprompter beam splitter glass”.
The majority of the one person founders quit on hardware.
Not due to the fact that the product is bad. It’s pretty difficult to obtain parts physically. Owners of a teleprompter need to get a teleprompter beam splitter glass to use a teleprompter. An injection molded enclosure needs to be custom made. Equipment for a set of mic mounts must be available. Evaluate the appeal of a part that you think of;

• Creating it and finding manufacturers that make it.

• Sending e-mails that don’t go to the recipient’s Inbox, and waiting for a reply for days.

• Please note their MOQs (Minimum Order Quantities) since they may be quite challenging for a new buyer.

• Determining which suppliers are and aren’t legitimate
This has been a time consuming process, usually done at trade shows and with numerous months of back and forth emailing, and a great deal of expensive pitfalls.
Axio Work is directly connected with the trusted suppliers of Alibaba.com globally. I made a prompt inquiring what things are available in the main Creator Dock and where they are available.
As I was filling my cup of coffee.
It provided him with real choices of providers. Pricing. MOQs. Verification status. I had data in front of me which I would have to gather by myself for weeks. Information which I spent weeks and weeks searching for, was there before me even had the chance to get out of the oven.
Was it perfect? No. I had some results which I felt that I didn’t getting the right results for my spec and was sifting the results. But if I didn’t have their support it wouldn’t be a few years back when I was not.
This is the highest point of altitude. Not perfection. Starting point.

Day 6: An application process I didn’t think would take 20 minutes, was 20 minutes!
It’ll just come out of my own ass. This is just devastating to finish competition applications.
You have a good idea of your proposal. You have details of the supplier. An idea for a business plan has come to you. Then you find you have to translate the whole thing into what are more or less boxes of text, and you find that it’s not exactly the way you thought when you read the application.
It was taken care of by the agent.
Filled out applications for each field automatically, based on our past proposal and sourcing information. I took the time to review, tweak a few things here and there that seemed a bit generic and copied this material into the form I was submitting it into.
From “let us do the application” to application submitting just a few hours (not days).
Suggest: Show the original application document on the left side, and the completed application document with each highlighted field on right side.

It Was Not Perfect is the subtitle of the book.
There’s a few things we need to straighten out here: The next step is not done with most AI content.
The financial forecasts that the agent originally created were too tidy. Too optimistic. The paper margins were quite good, with the exception of returns and shipping. There was a good paper margin, except for returns and shipping plus packaging and retail cut.
These had to be substituted by numbers which I worked out.
It’s no small feat. I would have been taken aback if I didn’t have as much experience as I do with the nuts and bolts of hardware unit economics, throwing numbers at a panel of real investors would have gotten me looking the fool.
Effectiveness of the agent will depend on the judgment exercised by the agent in trying to determine what the output of an agent is.
The section of the hype cycle that is always skipped: AI really enhances your starting point, but you still need to be familiar with the topic you’re writing about inside and out to ensure that it’s believable, not naïve.

What it is And it’s not what it is:
This is the simple put in the illustration.
AI is not “founder.” AI isn’t the ‘founder.’
Removes blank page. It dispels paralysis. It eliminates time spent formatting the questions, researching the questions and then filling a form, only to get to the ‘thinking’.
It’s up to you to think it out!
The conviction of the significance of this product. The decision as to what is defensible, and which isn’t. The people you can trust for the supplier and what they can expect.
It is still a “human task”! For now.
The “impossible to deal with” administration, however? That is gone. Or, that they are so out of it, it’s night and day.

A book to help you adapt to an uncertain future.
This book will help you prepare for an uncertain future.
A concept sketch of the Dashboard of an AI-powered Sourcing Tool that has a real-time availability of Suppliers, real-time Pricing changes, and a button to create a Purchase Order.
The 7 days we spend in the place will be 3 days in a year.
The sourcing side will be updated after 18 months while the sourcing proposal is being developed. Change in design specification and a change in the supplier options. The Budget component will automatically update when the Retail Price Target is changed.
The phrase “I have
